content: agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, and updating src/click/shell_completion.py to disable validation. Will coordinate on core.py edits.

Thanks. My changes: Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param + _close_callbacks/children tracking), Context.close()/__exit__ (validation logic), _make_sub_context (inherit validate_nesting), and shell_completion.py _resolve_context (set validate_nesting=False). I'll add the param at the end of __init__ signature to minimize conflicts with your on_exit param. Will do my core.py edits now - ping me if we collide.

FYI current core.py state (my validate_nesting edits are in): __init__ signature ends '...show_default: bool | None = None, validate_nesting: bool = False,) -> None:' - please add on_exit AFTER validate_nesting. Body: I added 'self.validate_nesting = validate_nesting' and 'self._children: list[Context] = []' right after 'self._exit_stack = ExitStack()' (~line 444-451) - add your on_exit assignment after that block. close() (~line 626): I added a validate_nesting check + RuntimeError raise BEFORE self._exit_stack.close(), and added parent._children cleanup AFTER the exit_stack reset (at the very end of the method) - please add your on_exit callback invocation in between (after _exit_stack.close()/reset, before or after my parent cleanup, doesn't matter which since they're independent). I'm done with my core.py edits now, just need to re-verify after you add yours. shell_completion.py changes are complete on my end (added validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context) - all yours if you need to touch it, just avoid those 3 call sites' new kwarg.

Status: I've finished my validate_nesting implementation in core.py and shell_completion.py, all tests pass (602 passed), and I've written patch.txt. My core.py edits are final on my end - feel free to layer on_exit on top whenever ready. No further changes planned from me unless you flag a conflict.
